params.name: Head of Hammurabi
params.description:
On Dec 1, 1982 BC, craftsmen carved the Head of Hammurabi.
This ancient Mesopotamian statue portrays a monarch of the time, possibly inspired by the renowned Babylonian king Hammurabi. The statue exhibits a realistic representation, featuring a royal cap and a serious and solemn expression. However, the artistic style suggests that it was likely crafted several hundred years prior to the Hammurabi era.
